tasks.description: Create history types file at `src/tools/video-editor/types/history.ts`. Define: `UndoSnapshot` ({config: TimelineConfig, signature: string}), `UndoEntry` ({snapshot, timestamp, label?, transactionId?}), `CheckpointTriggerType` union ('session_boundary' | 'edit_distance' | 'semantic' | 'manual'), `Checkpoint` ({id, timelineId, config, createdAt, triggerType, label, editsSinceLastCheckpoint}). Import TimelineConfig from existing types.
tasks.executor_notes: Created src/tools/video-editor/types/history.ts. Verified UndoSnapshot stores only {config: TimelineConfig, signature: string}; UndoEntry adds timestamp with optional label and transactionId; CheckpointTriggerType is limited to the four required values; and Checkpoint includes id, timelineId, config, createdAt, triggerType, label, and editsSinceLastCheckpoint.

tasks.description: Create Supabase migration for `timeline_checkpoints` table. Columns: id (UUID PK), timeline_id (UUID FK → timelines ON DELETE CASCADE), user_id (UUID FK → auth.users), config (JSONB NOT NULL), trigger_type (TEXT with CHECK constraint), label (TEXT NOT NULL), edits_since_last_checkpoint (INT DEFAULT 0), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()). Add index on (timeline_id, created_at DESC). Add RLS policy: users can only read/write/delete their own checkpoints (match user_id to auth.uid()).
tasks.executor_notes: Created supabase/migrations/20260326100500_create_timeline_checkpoints.sql. Verified the table includes the public.timelines FK with ON DELETE CASCADE, auth.users FK for user_id, trigger_type CHECK constraint covering the four allowed values, a composite index on (timeline_id, created_at DESC), and RLS policies enforcing auth.uid() = user_id for select/insert/update/delete.

tasks.description: Extend DataProvider interface (`src/tools/video-editor/data/DataProvider.ts`) with optional checkpoint methods: `saveCheckpoint?(timelineId: string, checkpoint: Omit<Checkpoint, 'id'>): Promise<string>` and `loadCheckpoints?(timelineId: string): Promise<Checkpoint[]>`. Implement in `SupabaseDataProvider.ts`: saveCheckpoint INSERTs and caps at 30 rows (delete oldest if exceeded), loadCheckpoint DELETEs rows older than 24h then SELECTs remaining ordered by created_at DESC.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended DataProvider with optional saveCheckpoint/loadCheckpoints methods and implemented both in SupabaseDataProvider. Verified saveCheckpoint inserts into timeline_checkpoints and then deletes the oldest rows beyond the 30-row cap for the same timeline/user. Verified loadCheckpoints deletes rows older than 24 hours before selecting the remaining checkpoints ordered by created_at descending.

tasks.description: Modify `commitData` in `useTimelineSave.ts` to support history. (1) Expand options type with `transactionId?: string`, `semantic?: boolean`, `skipHistory?: boolean`. (2) Add `onBeforeCommitRef` (MutableRefObject) to hook, called inside commitData before state update when `save !== false && !skipHistory`. Callback receives `(currentData, {transactionId, semantic})`. (3) Update `applyTimelineEdit` signature to accept and forward `transactionId` and `semantic`. (4) Update `applyResolvedConfigEdit` to accept and forward `semantic`. (5) Add explicit `skipHistory: true` to reloadFromServer (~line 436) and polled-data sync effect (~line 367). (6) Return `onBeforeCommitRef` from the hook.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated useTimelineSave so commitData accepts transactionId, semantic, and skipHistory; added onBeforeCommitRef; and verified the callback runs before dataRef.current is replaced, only when save is enabled and skipHistory is not set. Verified applyTimelineEdit forwards transactionId/semantic, applyResolvedConfigEdit now accepts semantic, and both the polled timeline sync path and reloadFromServer explicitly pass skipHistory: true.

tasks.description: Create `useTimelineHistory` hook at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts`. Core implementation: (1) Undo/redo stacks as refs, canUndo/canRedo as state updated on empty↔non-empty transitions. (2) `onBeforeCommit` callback snapshots `currentData.config` (NOT dataRef — avoids pre-mutation problem). (3) Transaction collapsing: skip push if transactionId matches top entry's; 300ms debounce for untransacted rapid edits. (4) Undo: pop from undo stack, push current config to redo, rebuild TimelineData via `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry(snapshot.config, dataRef.current)`, call `commitData(rebuilt, {save:true, skipHistory:true})`. Redo mirrors. (5) Checkpoint triggers inside onBeforeCommit: session boundary (>5min idle), edit-distance (30 edits), semantic flag. Fire-and-forget `provider.saveCheckpoint()`. (6) Load checkpoints on mount. (7) `jumpToCheckpoint`: restore config, clear both stacks. (8) `createManualCheckpoint`: save current config immediately. Cap undo at 100 entries.
tasks.executor_notes: Created src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.ts with undo/redo stacks held in refs and capped at 100, canUndo/canRedo state synced on empty/non-empty transitions, and an onBeforeCommit callback that snapshots currentData.config and signature before commits. Verified transaction collapsing handles exact transactionId matches separately from a 300ms untransacted debounce window, undo/redo rebuild via buildDataFromCurrentRegistry and call commitData with { save: true, skipHistory: true }, checkpoint triggers cover session boundary, edit distance, and semantic edits, checkpoints load on mount, jumpToCheckpoint clears both stacks before restoring, and createManualCheckpoint persists the current config immediately.

tasks.description: Wire `useTimelineHistory` into the data layer. (1) In `useTimelineData.ts`: instantiate the history hook, wire `save.onBeforeCommitRef.current = history.onBeforeCommit`. Update `UseTimelineDataResult` to include undo, redo, canUndo, canRedo, checkpoints, jumpToCheckpoint, createManualCheckpoint. (2) In `useTimelineState.types.ts`: add history fields to `TimelineChromeContextValue`. (3) In `useTimelineState.ts`: pass history fields from dataHook into the chrome memo.

Pass `transactionId` for drag operations in `useClipDrag.ts`: generate `crypto.randomUUID()` at drag session start, store on session object, pass `{transactionId}` in options to `applyTimelineEdit` at ~line 402. Same pattern in `useClipResize.ts`: generate at resize start, pass through all applyTimelineEdit calls during resize.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated drag and resize gesture paths to generate a transactionId at gesture start and reuse it for all applyTimelineEdit-backed mutations within that gesture. Verified useClipDrag stores one transactionId on the drag session and forwards it through same-track moves, cross-track moves that use moveClipToRow, and multi-clip applyTimelineEdit commits; verified useClipResize allocates one transactionId on resize start and forwards it on resize commit.

tasks.description: Pass `transactionId` for multi-clip keyboard track moves in `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts`. (1) Add optional `transactionId` param to `moveClipToRow` (~line 131) and forward to `applyTimelineEdit`. (2) In `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` (~line 296): generate single `crypto.randomUUID()` before the loop, pass to each `moveClipToRow` call. This ensures N clips moved by one keypress = 1 undo entry.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated useTimelineTrackManagement so moveClipToRow accepts and forwards an optional transactionId into applyTimelineEdit, and verified moveSelectedClipsToTrack generates one crypto.randomUUID() before the move loop and passes that same transactionId to every moveClipToRow call in the batch.

tasks.description: Mark destructive operations as semantic. (1) In `useClipEditing.ts` `handleDeleteClips`: pass `{semantic: true}` to `applyTimelineEdit`. (2) In `useTimelineTrackManagement.ts` `handleRemoveTrack` (~line 363): pass `{semantic: true}` to `applyResolvedConfigEdit`. (3) In `handleClearUnusedTracks` (~line 439): pass `{semantic: true}` to `applyResolvedConfigEdit`. Non-destructive callers (addTrack, moveTrack, trackPopoverChange, createTrackAndMoveClip) are NOT marked.
tasks.executor_notes: Marked only destructive operations as semantic. Verified handleDeleteClips passes { semantic: true } to applyTimelineEdit, handleRemoveTrack passes { semantic: true } to applyResolvedConfigEdit, and handleClearUnusedTracks passes { semantic: true } to applyResolvedConfigEdit; non-destructive track operations remain unchanged.

tasks.description: Add Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z keyboard shortcuts in `useKeyboardShortcuts.ts`. Add `undo` and `redo` to hook options. Add handlers early in the keydown listener (before existing checks) for meta/ctrl+Z (undo) and meta/ctrl+shift+Z or Y (redo), both with preventDefault. Wire in `VideoEditorShell.tsx` where useKeyboardShortcuts is called (~line 58): pass `chrome.undo` and `chrome.redo`.

Add undo/redo buttons and history dropdown to `VideoEditorShell.tsx`. (1) Undo/redo ghost buttons (h-6 w-6, Undo2/Redo2 icons) next to save badge, disabled when !canUndo/!canRedo. (2) History dropdown (shadcn DropdownMenu) with History icon trigger. Content: checkpoint list with label, trigger type badge, relative timestamp. Click → jumpToCheckpoint(id). Footer: 'Save checkpoint' button → createManualCheckpoint(). Empty state text. Import Undo2, Redo2, History from lucide-react.

Write unit tests for `useTimelineHistory` at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineHistory.test.ts`. 8 test cases: (1) basic undo/redo cycle (3 edits, undo 3x, redo 1x), (2) redo invalidation (undo then new edit clears redo), (3) transaction collapsing (5 entries same txnId = 1 undo entry), (4) 300ms debounce collapsing, (5) undo stack cap at 100, (6) checkpoint triggers (session boundary after 5min idle, edit distance at 30, semantic flag), (7) jumpToCheckpoint clears stacks, (8) skipHistory scenarios. Use mock commitData and mock DataProvider.
Run existing video editor test suite to verify no regressions from commitData/applyTimelineEdit signature changes. Fix any type errors in existing tests caused by the new optional parameters.
Deploy Supabase migration. Run `npx supabase db push --linked` to apply the timeline_checkpoints table and RLS policies. Verify table exists and policies are correct.

watch_items: SNAPSHOT CORRECTNESS: Snapshots must capture `currentData.config` (the last committed config), NOT `dataRef.current.rows/tracks`. Drop handlers (useAssetManagement.ts:164-165, useExternalDrop.ts:171-172, 197-202) mutate dataRef in-place before commitData — config is unaffected because it's rebuilt inside materializeData().

AGENT CHAT PATH (DECISION-004): Agent edits arrive via query invalidation/realtime polling (useAgentSession.ts:288-295, useTimelineRealtime.ts:27-47), NOT through applyResolvedConfigEdit. These correctly bypass undo (polling commits use skipHistory). Do NOT attempt to make agent edits undoable — treat them like external/collaborative edits.
DEBT: agent-chat-undo-integration — agent edits bypass undo stack by design. Do not make this worse by accidentally removing skipHistory from polling paths.

DEBT: conflict-resolution — silently reloading agent updates can discard dirty local edits. The checkpoint system gives users a recovery path but doesn't fix the root issue. Don't introduce new silent-reload paths.

PRE-MUTATION PROBLEM: Verify asset drop undo works correctly. The snapshot stores config from before the drop; on undo, buildDataFromCurrentRegistry rebuilds from that config, which should remove both the clip AND any auto-created track.

TRANSACTION COLLAPSING: Two mechanisms — exact transactionId match (drag, resize, multi-clip move) and 300ms time window (untransacted rapid edits like sliders). These are intentionally separate. Don't unify them.

CHECKPOINT EDIT COUNTING (minor flag): Transaction-collapsed gestures still count once per low-level commit toward the 30-edit auto-checkpoint cap. Acceptable for v1 — a drag of 20 intermediate positions counts as ~20 edits toward checkpoint threshold, not 1. Could refine later.

SKIP_HISTORY COMPLETENESS: reloadFromServer (~line 436) and polled-data sync effect (~line 367) in useTimelineSave.ts must have skipHistory:true. If any new commit path is added during implementation, evaluate whether it should skip history.

buildDataFromCurrentRegistry DEPENDENCY: Confirm this function exists in timeline-save-utils.ts and correctly rebuilds TimelineData from a config + current registry. It must preserve resolved URLs and registry entries.
RLS POLICY: timeline_checkpoints must filter by user_id = auth.uid(). Users should never see other users' checkpoints even if they share a timeline_id.
UNDO STACK SIZE: 100 entries × ~10-20KB config = ~1-2MB. Monitor in practice. If configs grow larger (many clips), consider structural sharing or reducing cap.
canUndo/canRedo STATE TRANSITIONS: Only update React state on empty↔non-empty transitions to avoid re-rendering the entire editor tree on every edit. Do not use derived state from stack length.

sense_checks.question: Does onBeforeCommit snapshot `currentData.config` (not dataRef.current.rows/tracks)? Does transaction collapsing check both exact transactionId match AND 300ms debounce (separately)? Does undo call commitData with `{save: true, skipHistory: true}`? Do checkpoint triggers cover all 3 types (session boundary, edit distance, semantic)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed onBeforeCommit snapshots currentData.config, transaction collapsing handles exact transactionId matches separately from the 300ms debounce path, undo/redo restore via commitData({ save: true, skipHistory: true }), and checkpoint triggers cover session boundary, edit distance, and semantic edits.

sense_checks.question: Is `save.onBeforeCommitRef.current = history.onBeforeCommit` wired in useTimelineData? Are all 7 history fields (undo, redo, canUndo, canRedo, checkpoints, jumpToCheckpoint, createManualCheckpoint) threaded through types → state → chrome context?
Is a new transactionId generated at the START of each drag/resize session (not per frame)? Is the same transactionId passed to every applyTimelineEdit call within that gesture?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed drag and resize generate a transactionId at gesture start and reuse that same id for every applyTimelineEdit-backed mutation in the gesture.

sense_checks.question: Does moveClipToRow accept and forward an optional transactionId? Does moveSelectedClipsToTrack generate ONE transactionId before the loop and pass it to every moveClipToRow call?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed moveClipToRow now accepts and forwards an optional transactionId, and moveSelectedClipsToTrack creates one transactionId before the loop and passes it to every moveClipToRow call.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed only handleDeleteClips, handleRemoveTrack, and handleClearUnusedTracks are marked semantic; addTrack, moveTrack, trackPopoverChange, and createTrackAndMoveClip were left non-semantic.

Are undo/redo buttons correctly disabled when stacks are empty? Does the history dropdown show checkpoints with labels, trigger type badges, and relative timestamps? Is there a 'Save checkpoint' action and an empty state?

Do tests cover all 8 specified cases? Do transaction collapsing tests verify that 5 entries with same txnId produce exactly 1 undo entry? Does the checkpoint trigger test verify saveCheckpoint is called with the correct trigger_type?

meta_commentary:
**Execution guidance:**

**Critical path:** T1+T2 (parallel) → T3+T4 (parallel, T3 needs T1+T2, T4 needs T1) → T5 (needs T3+T4) → T6 (needs T5) → T7+T8+T9+T10+T11+T12 (parallel, various deps). T13 is the integration gate. T14 is deploy.

**The single hardest thing:** T5 (useTimelineHistory) is the bulk of the work. Read `useTimelineSave.ts` thoroughly first — understand `commitData`, `materializeData`, `applyTimelineEdit`, and `applyResolvedConfigEdit` call flow. The snapshot strategy (config-based, not dataRef-based) is the key design decision. Get this right and everything else follows.

**Pre-mutation problem (most subtle bug risk):** Drop handlers in useAssetManagement and useExternalDrop mutate `dataRef.current.tracks` and `dataRef.current.rows` IN PLACE before calling applyTimelineEdit. The onBeforeCommit callback receives `dataRef.current` which already has these mutations. BUT we only read `.config` and `.signature` from it — config is rebuilt by `materializeData()` inside `applyTimelineEdit`, so it still reflects the PREVIOUS committed state. This is why we snapshot config, not rows/tracks.

**Agent chat edits (corrected assumption):** The plan originally claimed agent edits participate in undo via applyResolvedConfigEdit. This is WRONG — agent edits arrive via query invalidation/realtime (useAgentSession.ts:288-295), which triggers polling, which commits with skipHistory. This is actually correct behavior: externally-originated edits should not be undoable. Do not attempt to wire agent edits into the undo system.

**Checkpoint edit counting (known minor imprecision):** During a drag, each intermediate position counts as 1 edit toward the 30-edit checkpoint cap, even though transaction collapsing means they're 1 undo entry. This is acceptable for v1 — it means checkpoints trigger slightly more often during heavy drag sessions, which is harmless.

**buildDataFromCurrentRegistry:** Verify this function exists in timeline-save-utils.ts before writing T5. It's referenced in the plan but needs confirmation. It should take a TimelineConfig + current TimelineData and rebuild rows/meta/clipOrder/tracks/output from the config while preserving the registry and resolved URLs.

**Deploy order:** Do NOT deploy the migration (T14) until all code changes pass tests (T13). The migration creates the table but existing code won't break without it — the DataProvider methods are optional.

**Supabase safety:** Deploy with `npx supabase db push --linked` (never `db reset --linked`). Deploy functions individually if any edge functions are touched (none in this plan).
